title.en: A Guided Tour of the Annexes: PICS, MIB, Regulatory Tables, and Worked Examples
title.zh: 附录导览：PICS、MIB、监管表格与示例附录

blocks.en: If the numbered clauses of IEEE 802.11-2024 are the rulebook, the annexes at the very back are the appendices the rulebook leans on — the conformance checklist, the catalogue of manageable settings, the per-country channel tables, and a generous pile of worked examples. The standard runs roughly 4900 pages; the annexes occupy the last thousand or so. You almost never read them cover to cover. Instead you learn what each one is for, so that when a question comes up you know exactly which appendix to flip to.
blocks.zh: 如果说 IEEE 802.11-2024 编号清晰的正文条款是规则手册，那么最末尾的附录就是这本手册所依赖的附加资料——一致性核对清单、可管理设置的目录、各国信道表格，以及一大堆翔实的示例。整部标准约有 4900 页，附录占据了最后约一千页。你几乎不会从头读到尾，而是去搞清楚每个附录各自的用途，这样一旦遇到问题，就能准确地知道该翻到哪一个附录。

blocks.en: The single most important habit is to read the parenthetical label right under each annex heading. Every annex says either "(normative)" or "(informative)". Normative means it carries requirements you must meet to claim conformance — it has the same force as the main body. Informative means it is there to help you understand or check your work, but does not add obligations. So Annex B (PICS), Annex C (MIB), Annex D (regulatory references), and Annex E (country/operating classes) are normative; the long tail of example annexes — Annex G, I, J and many more — is informative.
blocks.zh: 最重要的一个习惯，就是读每个附录标题正下方那对括号里的标签。每个附录都会标注 "(normative)"（规范性）或 "(informative)"（资料性）。规范性意味着它承载着你必须满足、才能声称符合标准的要求——其效力与正文相同。资料性则意味着它的作用是帮助你理解或核对自己的工作，但不增加任何义务。因此 Annex B（PICS）、Annex C（MIB）、Annex D（监管引用）和 Annex E（国家/操作等级）都是规范性的；而长长一串示例附录——Annex G、I、J 以及更多——则都是资料性的。

blocks.en: Quick rule of thumb: "(normative)" = you can fail conformance by ignoring it; "(informative)" = you can ignore it and still conform, but you'd be throwing away free help. When in doubt, check the parenthetical before you trust an annex as a requirement.
blocks.zh: 一个快速的经验法则："(normative)" = 忽略它你就可能通不过一致性检查；"(informative)" = 忽略它你仍然合规，但等于白白丢掉了现成的帮助。拿不准时，先看那对括号，再决定是否把某个附录当作硬性要求来对待。

blocks.en:
mindmap
  root((802.11-2024 Annexes))
    Normative
      B PICS proforma
        conformance checklist
        M / O status symbols
      C MIB in ASN.1
        managed objects catalog
      D Regulatory references
        Table D-1 sources
      E Country & operating classes
        Table E-1 channels/power
    Informative
      A Bibliography
      G Frame-exchange sequences
        EBNF grammar
      I OFDM frame-encoding examples
      J RSNA reference code + test vectors
      N..Z misc examples
blocks.zh:
mindmap
  root((802.11-2024 附录))
    规范性
      B PICS 一致性声明
        实现核对清单
        M / O 状态符号
      C MIB（ASN.1）
        受管对象目录
      D 监管引用
        表 D-1 来源
      E 国家与操作等级
        表 E-1 信道/功率
    资料性
      A 参考文献
      G 帧交换序列
        EBNF 文法
      I OFDM 帧编码示例
      J RSNA 参考代码 + 测试向量
      N..Z 各类示例

blocks.en: Start with the four normative anchors. Annex B is the PICS proforma — a Protocol Implementation Conformance Statement: a giant fill-in questionnaire where a vendor declares, line by line, which capabilities and options they implement, marking each item M (mandatory), O (optional), or conditional. It is the official way to state what a product does. Annex C is the MIB written in ASN.1 — the formal catalogue of every managed object (the dot11... attributes you can read or set). Annex D collects the external regulatory references (its Table D-1 points to the actual government rules), and Annex E turns regulation into engineering: its country elements and operating-class tables (Table E-1 onward) map channels, bandwidths, and power limits to legal operation in each country.
blocks.zh: 先从四个规范性支柱入手。Annex B 是 PICS 一致性声明（Protocol Implementation Conformance Statement）：一份庞大的填空式问卷，厂商在其中逐行声明自己实现了哪些能力和可选项，每一项都标注为 M（强制）、O（可选）或带条件。这是声明某款产品做了什么的官方方式。Annex C 是用 ASN.1 写成的 MIB——把每一个受管对象（你可以读取或设置的那些 dot11... 属性）都正式编入目录。Annex D 汇集外部监管引用（其表 D-1 指向真正的政府法规），而 Annex E 则把监管转化为工程：它的国家信息元素和操作等级表（从表 E-1 起）将信道、带宽和功率上限映射到各个国家的合法运行方式。

blocks.en: Now the friendlier half — the informative example annexes, which exist precisely so a beginner can check understanding against known-good answers. Annex G writes out the allowable frame-exchange sequences as an EBNF grammar, so you can confirm whether a sequence of frames you observed is legal. Annex I walks through encoding a frame for the OFDM PHY end to end — every preamble, scrambling, coding and modulation step shown with concrete numbers. Annex J gives RSNA reference implementations in C plus test vectors, so a security implementer can run the published inputs and confirm their code produces the published outputs. Several more (Annex N, O and others) follow the same spirit. The beginner's move is not to read these straight through, but to use them as an answer key: implement from the clauses, then verify against the worked example.
blocks.zh: 再看更友好的另一半——资料性示例附录，它们的存在正是为了让初学者拿自己的理解去和已知正确的答案做对照。Annex G 用 EBNF 文法写出所有允许的帧交换序列，于是你可以核实自己观察到的一串帧是否合法。Annex I 端到端地演示如何为 OFDM PHY 编码一个帧——前导、加扰、编码、调制的每一步都配有具体数字。Annex J 给出用 C 语言写的 RSNA 参考实现外加测试向量，安全功能的实现者可以喂入这些公开的输入，确认自己的代码产出与公开的输出一致。还有若干附录（Annex N、O 等）也秉持同样的精神。初学者的正确做法不是把它们从头读一遍，而是把它们当作标准答案：先依据正文条款实现，再拿这些示例来验证。

blocks.en: A practical reading loop: (1) implement or reason from the numbered clause; (2) if it is testable, find the matching informative annex — G for frame sequences, I for OFDM encoding, J for RSNA crypto — and run your output against its vectors; (3) when you ship, fill in the Annex B PICS to declare conformance, and consult Annex E for the channels/power your target country actually allows.
blocks.zh: 一个实用的阅读循环：(1) 依据编号条款进行实现或推理；(2) 若它可被测试，就找到对应的资料性附录——帧序列看 G、OFDM 编码看 I、RSNA 密码学看 J——把你的输出与其测试向量对照；(3) 准备发布时，填写 Annex B 的 PICS 来声明一致性，并查阅 Annex E，确认目标国家实际允许的信道与功率。

summary.en: Exactly what the PICS proforma is, and the M/O status symbols
summary.zh: PICS 一致性声明究竟是什么，以及 M/O 状态符号

blocks.en: Annex B opens (B.1) by stating that a supplier claiming compliance with IEEE Std 802.11-2024 shall complete the PICS proforma, and that the completed proforma is the PICS for that implementation — a statement of which capabilities and options have been implemented. It lists four uses: a checklist for the implementer, a capability indication for acquirers, an interworking pre-check for users (incompatible PICS often predict failure to interwork), and a basis for a tester to select tests. B.2.1 defines the Status-column symbols: M = mandatory, O = optional, O.<n> = support of at least one of a numbered group is required, and pred: = a predicate identification (conditional). B.3.1 explains the questionnaire layout: each item has a reference, a question, the clause references that specify it, the status, and a Support column the supplier marks (typically Yes/No/N/A). Marking an item supported asserts that all relevant requirements of the cited subclauses and normative annexes are met — which is why the parenthetical normative/informative label on each annex matters here too.
blocks.zh: Annex B 在开篇（B.1）即声明：凡声称符合 IEEE Std 802.11-2024 的供应商都应当填写 PICS 一致性声明，且填写完成的声明就是该实现的 PICS——即对已实现了哪些能力与可选项的陈述。它列出四种用途：作为实现者的核对清单、向采购方表明能力、供用户做互通预检（不兼容的 PICS 往往预示互通会失败），以及作为测试者挑选测试用例的依据。B.2.1 定义了状态列的符号：M = 强制，O = 可选，O.<n> = 同一编号分组中至少需支持其一，pred: = 谓词标识（带条件）。B.3.1 说明问卷布局：每一项都有项目编号、问题、规定该项的条款引用、状态，以及供供应商勾选的 Support（支持）列（通常为 Yes/No/N/A）。把某项标为已支持，即断言所引用子条款及规范性附录的全部相关要求均已满足——这也正是为什么每个附录上那对规范性/资料性括号标签在此同样重要。

cites.note: Annex A (informative) Bibliography start; Annex B (normative) PICS proforma start — the parenthetical normative/informative label convention
B.1 PICS purpose and uses; B.2.1 M/O/O.<n>/pred status symbols; B.3.1 questionnaire structure
Annex C (normative) ASN.1 encoding of the MAC and PHY MIB — catalogue of managed objects
Annex D (normative) Regulatory references with Table D-1; Annex E (normative) Country elements and operating classes (channel/power tables, Table E-1 onward)
Informative worked-example annexes: G frame-exchange sequences (EBNF); I OFDM/DMG/EDMG frame-encoding examples; J RSNA reference implementations and test vectors
